researched_category.notes: Binance is the world's largest cryptocurrency exchange by trading volume. Their developer center lists products across Spot, Derivatives, Margin, Algo Trading, Wallet, and more.

one_liner.value: Provides REST and WebSocket APIs for crypto trading, market data, account management, wallet operations, staking, and other exchange services.

methods.notes: Primary auth is API key + secret key passed via X-MBX-APIKEY header, with HMAC SHA256, Ed25519, or RSA signature. Binance OAuth (OAuth 2.0) also exists for third-party data sharing. Public market data endpoints need no auth. Custom refers to the signature-based auth (HMAC/Ed25519/RSA) which is a custom scheme on top of API key.

access.notes: API keys are self-serve but require: (1) account registration with identity verification (KYC - government ID, liveness check), (2) two-factor authentication (2FA), (3) a minimum deposit to Spot Wallet. KYC is mandatory for API key creation. Some institutional features (OMS Toolkit, VIP) require sales contact. The API itself is free to use with standard rate limits (1200 weight/min default).

summary.value: Binance has extensive, well-documented public REST and WebSocket APIs covering Spot, Futures, Margin, Options, Wallet, Staking, Mining, Loans, Sub-accounts, Pay, Gift Cards, NFT, and more. Also offers WebSocket streams for real-time data, FIX API, and Binance OAuth. Public market data endpoints are available without authentication. The developer portal at developers.binance.com offers comprehensive documentation, SDKs, and changelogs.
summary.notes: The API is one of the most comprehensive in the crypto space. REST API uses weight-based rate limiting (default 1200/min). GraphQL is not explicitly documented but WebSocket streaming is well-supported. CLI/SDK tools exist through community libraries.

agent_interface.notes: No official MCP server from Binance. Multiple community MCP servers exist: AnalyticAce/binance-mcp-server (Python, 48 stars), ethancod1ng/binance-mcp-server (TypeScript), snjyor/binance-mcp, TermiX-official/binance-mcp, and others on mcpmarket.com and LangDB. No Composio-specific Binance toolkit found.

rationale.value: Binance is buildable as an agent toolkit today. It has a comprehensive, well-documented public REST and WebSocket API with self-serve API key generation (requires KYC which is standard for financial services). Multiple community MCP servers already exist demonstrating feasibility. The API supports all standard trading operations, market data retrieval, and account management. The primary authentication (API key + HMAC/Ed25519 signature) is straightforward to implement programmatically. The main consideration is that users need a Binance account with completed identity verification and a small deposit, which is a reasonable prerequisite for a financial trading tool.
rationale.notes: Binance is one of the most integrated crypto exchanges in the developer ecosystem. Multiple community MCP servers already exist and work. The API documentation is comprehensive. The only 'gate' is standard KYC compliance required by financial regulations.

s1.quote: Developer Center - Spot Trading, Derivatives Trading, Margin Trading, Algo Trading, Wallet, Copy Trading, Convert, Alpha Trading, Sub Account, Staking, Mining, Crypto Loan, Binance Pay, Gift Card, etc.
s1.title: Binance Developer Center

s2.quote: API-keys are passed into the REST API via the X-MBX-APIKEY header. API-keys and secret-keys are case-sensitive. Security Types: NONE, TRADE, USER_DATA, USER_STREAM, MARKET_DATA.
s2.title: Binance.US API Documentation

s3.quote: Please note that before creating an API Key, you need to: enable two-factor authentication (2FA) on your account; make a deposit of any amount to your Spot Wallet to activate your account; and verify your identity.
s3.title: How to Create API Keys on Binance?

s4.quote: We recommend to use Ed25519 API keys. HMAC keys use symmetric cryptography... HMAC keys are deprecated. We recommend to migrate to asymmetric API keys, such as Ed25519 or RSA.
s4.title: binance-spot-api-docs/faqs/api_key_types.md

s5.quote: Binance OAuth allows users to share specific data with a 3rd application while keeping their accounts' API keys or login credentials. The Binance API allows developers to use the OAuth 2.0 protocol.
s5.title: Binance OAuth Landing Page

s6.quote: Market Data Only URLs - These URLs do not require any authentication (i.e. The API key is not necessary) and serve only public market data.
s6.title: Market Data Only URLs

s7.quote: Identity verification (KYC) protects against fraud... New users must complete [Verified] verification to access Binance's full range of products and services.
s7.title: Complete Guidelines of Identity Verification on Binance

s8.quote: Binance announcement on August 6, 2021: 'To remain compliant with the latest industry requirements, Binance is updating its API services to limit new API key creation by accounts that have only completed basic account verification.'
s8.title: Intermediate KYC becomes required to trade with Kryll on Binance via the API

s9.quote: Rule 1: API keys without an IP restriction are automatically deleted after 90 days of creation or 30 days of inactivity. Rule 2: Withdrawal permissions cannot be enabled at all unless the API key has at least one IP address whitelisted.
s9.title: Binance API IP Whitelist: Fix Key Expiry and Enable Withdrawals on Cloud Platforms

s10.quote: SIGNED request example (HMAC), SIGNED request example (RSA), and SIGNED request example (Ed25519). Timing security: timestamp and recvWindow validation.
s10.title: General Info - Binance Developer Docs

s11.quote: A powerful Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that enables AI agents to interact seamlessly with the Binance cryptocurrency exchange. This server provides a comprehensive suite of trading tools, market data access, and account management capabilities.
s11.title: AnalyticAce/binance-mcp-server

s12.quote: A Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for integrating AI assistants with Binance cryptocurrency exchange APIs, enabling automated trading, market data access, and account management.
s12.title: ethancod1ng/binance-mcp-server

s13.quote: Binance Cryptocurrency MCP - enables AI agents to access real-time Binance cryptocurrency market data including prices, order books, candlestick charts, trading history, and 24-hour statistics.
s13.title: snjyor/binance-mcp

limitations: The developers.binance.com general info page could not be fetched directly (fetch error), but equivalent info was obtained from docs.binance.us and other Binance documentation pages.
No official Binance MCP server exists - only community builds. However, this is not a blocker for buildability.
Composio does not appear to have a dedicated Binance toolkit in its catalogue (they have Coinbase, CoinMarketCap, Coinranking, etc.)
Binance.US API is documented separately from Binance.com API; this analysis covers both as they share the same authentication model.
Some institutional features (OMS Toolkit, VIP) require sales contact, but the core trading/market data APIs are self-serve.
